_note: Batch 9, sub-batch 1 of ~4 (research batches of 15 per user instruction). Two categories: mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries (Batch A -- Israel-based post-high-school yeshivot/seminaries not already in the DB, found via the actual joint-application directories yeshivaapplication.org and applytosem.org) and mechinot_batch1 (first 10 of ~43 Israeli pre-military mechinot identified as JCM (Joint Council of Pre-Military Academies) members not already in the DB, via mechinot.org.il's phonebook directory). Per explicit user policy: every field sourced from each program's own official site only; no price/tuition/fees; no specific session dates (only coarse durationType + free-text durationText where a program states its own length, e.g. 'gap year'); null/empty left wherever the official site did not state something -- nothing inferred or guessed. Duplicate-avoidance: user personally confirmed Otzem(JCM)=existing otzem-overseas-program, Beit Yisrael(JCM)=existing mechinat-beit-yisrael-big, Maayanot=existing mayanot-womens-program (distinct from existing machon-maayan), and directed adding Otzem's two branch programs (Ke'Ayal, Midreshet Rina) which will appear in a later sub-batch. Ateret Yerushalayim(JCM) deliberately excluded as a likely-duplicate of existing yeshivat-ateret-yerushalayim -- Claude's judgment call per user request, on grounds of exact name match plus existing precedent for one institution carrying both a yeshiva/hesder identity and a mechina identity on a single row (e.g. Ein Prat Academy is tagged both mechina and pre-army).

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Yeshivat Torah V'Avodah (YTVA)
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A hesder-recognized yeshiva for male high school graduates from around the world, combining rigorous Torah study with competitive internships and volunteer work with Israeli organizations. Runs a high-level Kollel program on campus; graduates have enlisted in elite IDF combat units including paratroop units.

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Midreshet Torah V'Avodah (MTVA)
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A midrasha for female high school graduates combining rigorous Torah study with competitive Israeli internships and volunteer work, plus a Machon Leadership Program offering Israel-advocacy training and meetings with political and military leaders.

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Yeshiva Tiferet (TJ)
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A post-high-school yeshiva in the Jerusalem suburb of Bayit Vegan, offering five distinct shiur platforms to accommodate students from beginner to advanced Talmudic study levels, with residential accommodations, weekly Shabbat programming with faculty mentors, and joint academic-credit programs with Yeshiva University, Touro College, and Hebrew Theological College.

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Yeshivat Yishrei Lev
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A classical Talmud-focused yeshiva for post-high-school students from North America, emphasizing intensive Talmudic analysis (iyun and bekiyus), Halacha, Jewish philosophy (machshava), and character development (mussar), with multi-year Shana Aleph/Bet/Gimel tracks.

mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.name: Midreshet Lev
mens_womens_yeshivot_seminaries.description: A women's seminary in Ramat Beit Shemesh offering advanced Torah classes in Gemara, Halacha, Tanach, Machshava, and Chassidut, combined with Religious Zionist education, personalized mentorship through rakezet (advisor) relationships, and chesed/community-engagement programming.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Avner - Akko
mechinot_batch1.description: A religious pre-military academy in Akko offering 12 to 18 months of study before graduates enlist for military service. Established in 2014 and named after Avner Shimon Gatz, a soldier killed in the battle to liberate the Western Wall in the Six-Day War; founded by his nephew, Rabbi Ahikam Gatz.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Orot
mechinot_batch1.description: A Torah-based pre-military academy in the settlement of Talmon, established in 2008, affiliated with Yeshivat Hakotel. Focuses on connecting students to Torah study (Talmud, faith, Jewish law) at an individualized pace; admission is based on interview and personal acquaintance rather than academic credentials alone.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Eitan
mechinot_batch1.location: Ma'ale Adumim and Mevuot Jericho, Israel
mechinot_batch1.description: A pre-military academy established in 2010 as an initiative of the Beit Morasha association, combining Torah study with practical work, manual labor, and direct connection to the land. Operates at two locations with roughly 150 first- and second-year students combined.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Elisha
mechinot_batch1.description: A veteran yeshiva-style pre-military academy adjacent to Neve Tzuf in the Binyamin area, aiming to develop religious soldiers with a firm Torah grounding. Also runs a post-military beit midrash track for army graduates continuing traditional study while addressing life skills and spiritual development.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Arzei HaLevanon
mechinot_batch1.description: A Torah-based and Zionist pre-military academy based in the Shiloh settlement, which also operates a program in Beit She'an and a newer track, Chavat Zion, in Jaffa.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Beit Yatir
mechinot_batch1.location: Beit Yatir, southern Mount Hebron, Israel
mechinot_batch1.description: A pre-military academy established in 1991 combining deep Torah study, military preparation, agriculture, and settlement in the natural setting of the Yatir forest. Roughly 100 students total across first and second year. Selection emphasizes genuine motivation over academic or religious credentials.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Chosen Peduel
mechinot_batch1.description: A pre-military academy in the Peduel settlement emphasizing student self-governance and social leadership, combining Jewish studies in the spirit of Rabbi Kook and Chassidut with practical military preparation -- physical training, navigation, survival training, firearms instruction, and spoken Arabic lessons coordinated with IDF intelligence units. Over 1,500 graduates have gone on to serve as IDF officers and other roles in Israeli society.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Chemdat Yehuda
mechinot_batch1.description: A religious pre-military academy in the Chemdat settlement, founded in 1997 by Rabbi Yinon Meder, training students for meaningful life and military service.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Yedidya
mechinot_batch1.description: A post-high-school, pre-army program in Beit Shemesh for graduates of religious high schools from Israel and abroad, preparing young men emotionally, spiritually, psychologically, and physically for military service and citizenship. Established 2011; recognized and funded by Israel's Ministry of Education and Ministry of Defense.

mechinot_batch1.name: Mechinat Yonatan
mechinot_batch1.description: A religious pre-military academy for both young men and women, named for Yoni Netanyahu z"l, established by the Religious Kibbutz Movement at Kibbutz Alumim. Combines Torah, faith, and Jewish philosophy study with kibbutz labor (packing facility, field crops, orchards, kitchen, ranch work), community volunteering, and military preparation. Opened an additional branch at Kibbutz Sa'ad in 2024.
